channel.title: Canned Publishing – English
channel.description: Svensk politik och nyheter, flerspråkigt och i klarspråk.

item.title: The ECB (European Central Bank) reports on the progress made towards the introduction of the euro.
item.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) reports that countries seeking to adopt the euro are facing difficulties in meeting the necessary criteria. Wars and global uncertainty are hindering the economic development of these countries, preventing th

item.title: Iceland joins TIPS for instant payments.
item.description: Island has signed an agreement that will allow for the direct transfer of funds in Icelandic krona, available 24/7. This will become effective in 2028 through a new system for fast payments.

item.title: Statements from Commissioner Hoekstra regarding the proposal for simplified tax rules.
item.description: The European Commission aims to make it easier for businesses to operate by simplifying tax regulations.

item.title: Remarks by the Executive Vice-President Virkkunen and Commissioners Brunner and McGrath regarding the strengthening of Europol and Eurojust.
item.description: The EU has decided on new regulations to strengthen the authorities Europol and Eurojust. This is intended to help combat crime in Europe more effectively.

item.title: Daily News, 2026-06-24.
item.description: The EU plans to borrow up to 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026 by issuing new bonds, known as EU bonds.

item.title: The European Commission will disburse 846 million euros to Spain to support the recovery efforts following the floods in the Valencia region in 2024.
item.description: The European Union is providing €846 million to Spain to help rebuild the Valencia region following the major floods of 2024.

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the state's governance of municipalities' exercise of authority, specifically concerning the LSS (Law on Support and Service for Persons with Disabilities).
item.description: The Swedish National Audit Office has reviewed how the state ensures that municipalities comply with the LSS Act, which provides support to people with disabilities. They believe that the state's efforts are not effective enough and want to

item.title: Follow-up on the crime prevention strategy "Barriers Against Crime."
item.description: The government has conducted a follow-up on its plan to combat crime. They believe that the new methods are helping to reduce recruitment into criminal gangs.

item.title: Follow-up of the national strategy to combat organized crime.
item.description: The government has released a report detailing the efforts to combat gangs and organized crime in Sweden. The report states that the work in this area has improved, but that crime remains a significant threat to society.

item.title: Philip R. Lane: Introductory remarks.
item.description: The situation in the Middle East and high energy prices are creating uncertainty for Europe's economy. To protect the economy from rising prices, a slight interest rate increase has been implemented.

item.title: The Commission is simplifying and making energy labeling and tire labeling more accessible for European consumers.
item.description: The EU wants to make it easier for everyone to understand the labeling on tires and appliances. This is intended to help people make better choices when they are shopping.

item.title: The Commission plans to issue EU bonds with a value of 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026.
item.description: The EU plans to borrow 80 billion euros during the second half of 2026. This is part of a larger plan to borrow a total of 180 billion euros throughout the entire year.

item.title:
New political direction for a stronger film industry.
# A New Political Direction for a Stronger Film Industry
* **A clear vision:** We need a clear vision for the future of the film industry. * **Increased funding:** We need to increase funding for film production and distribution. * **Support for independent filmmakers:** We need to provide more support for independent filmmakers. * **Promotion of diversity and inclusion:** We need to promote diversity and inclusion in the film industry. * **International collaboration:** We need to strengthen international collaboration in the film industry.
This new political direction will help to create a stronger and more vibrant film industry in Sweden. It will also help to promote Swedish culture and values around the world.
We believe that this new political direction is essential for the future of the film industry. We are confident that it will help to create a more sustainable and successful industry for years to come.
item.description: The government's proposal outlines new goals for national film policy. Based on the national cultural policy objectives, the national film policy should contribute to the creation and distribution of

item.title: Christine Lagarde: Hearing before the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs at the European Parliament.
item.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) is raising interest rates to curb rising prices. This measure is being taken to protect the economy in the wake of the war in the Middle East, which has made energy more expensive.

item.title: Frank Elderson: A relaxed Q&A session.
item.description: Frank Elderson, from the European Central Bank (ECB), explains how banking regulations can be simplified without compromising the stability of banks. The goal is to strengthen Europe's economy and make it easier for banks to provide loans f

item.title: Bill: Tougher penalties for organised crime
item.description: The government proposes higher minimum sentences and expanded powers for covert surveillance in cases of organised crime.

item.title: Sweden's foreign intelligence service
item.description: Sweden is establishing a new authority – Sweden's Foreign Intelligence Service (UND) – which is to begin operating on 1 January 2027. The government proposes a new law on how the authority may process personal data, together with consequential amendments to more than twenty other laws so that they work together with the new authority.

item.title: City Council, City of Stockholm, 2026-06-17
item.description: The City Council of Stockholm has responded to questions regarding traffic changes, addressed a legal review of the Slussen area, and provided feedback on proposals concerning how personal data can be used to prevent crime.

item.title: Stronger protection for the independence of courts and judges
item.description: The government wants to strengthen the courts' independence from political influence through a new, consolidated law on courts and judges. The National Courts Administration becomes more independent and is renamed the Courts Board, and clearer safeguards are introduced for how judges are appointed and can be removed from office. Most of the rules are proposed to take effect on 1 April 2027.

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Agency's report on the Swedish Migration Agency's controls of work permits – a high risk of errors and abuse.
item.description: The Swedish National Audit Agency has identified significant shortcomings in how the Swedish Migration Agency controls work permits. There is a high risk that errors and fraud related to work permits will not be detected, which could lead t

item.title: Youth Policy Document
item.description: The government has published a report on the well-being of young people in Sweden today. The goal is for all young people to thrive, be able to influence society, and have a secure future.

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on infiltration within the government.
item.description: The Swedish National Audit Office has investigated how three government agencies are working to prevent infiltration, which refers to attempts by external parties to improperly influence the agencies. The findings indicate that the agencies

item.title: Hearing and temporary transfer in arrest warrant cases
item.description: The government wants to amend the rules on how Sweden surrenders wanted persons under a European or Nordic arrest warrant, in order to clarify them and align them with the case law of the EU Court of Justice. Among other things, a wanted person should be able to be heard or temporarily transferred for trial, and in certain cases the issuing country's consent will be required. The amendments are proposed to apply from 1 December 2026.

item.title: City Council, Stockholm Municipality, June 15, 2026.
item.description: The City of Stockholm has decided on new regulations regarding child safety in municipal preschools, as well as new fees for preschool, after-school care, and childcare services offered during inconvenient hours.

item.title: Denied Forced Care – Found Dead in the Snow
item.description: A mentally ill 28-year-old, Lina Bryant, was refused compulsory psychiatric care and made to leave in the middle of the night – shortly afterwards she was found dead in the snow in Tanum municipality, Sweden. Despite being in a psychotic state and warnings from her family, she received no help. Her mother is now demanding change.

item.title: Attempted Decapitation Occurred in Belfast
item.description: A man – reported to be a Scottish man in his 40s, Stephen Ogilvie – was the target of an attempted beheading on an open street in north Belfast. A Sudanese man has been charged with attempted murder. The attack took place in a deprived area with a long history of violence and tensions over migration.

item.title: Saint Peter of Sävsjön Became the Pope of Crewe
item.description: Ten members of a Shia Muslim sect have been charged in the United Kingdom with offences including human trafficking, forced marriage and rape. The sect – The Ahmadi Religion of Peace and Light – previously had its headquarters in Sävsjön outside Hällefors in Sweden, where locals remember the members as extremely secretive.

item.title: More efficient control options in systems for root cause analysis, risk and uncertainty management, green technology, and personnel records.
item.description: The Swedish Tax Agency (Skatteverket) wants to gain better control over fraud related to tax deductions for things like cleaning services and solar panels. The proposal would make it easier for the agency to audit companies and detect viola

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the Swedish Social Insurance Agency's and the Swedish Pensions Agency's handling of changing circumstances.
item.description: The Swedish National Audit Agency has identified shortcomings in how the Swedish Social Insurance Agency (Försäkringskassan) and the Swedish Pensions Agency (Pensionsmyndigheten) handle changes in circumstances, which can lead to incorrect

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the handling of cases involving individuals who do not speak Swedish.
item.description: The government has reviewed a report that demonstrates how government agencies can improve their communication with individuals who do not speak Swedish. The goal is to ensure that everyone understands the decisions they receive from the st

item.title: Personnel supply for the military defense.
item.description: To strengthen the defense, mandatory military service is being extended in certain situations. The period of service in the Swedish Armed Forces' reserve, designated for potential deployment in wartime, will now extend until the year a pers

item.title: Technical review of the value-added tax (VAT) rules for cross-border trade.
item.description: The government proposes new rules for VAT (Value Added Tax) in cross-border trade to comply with the EU's new laws. The proposal aims to clarify how taxes should be paid in the context of digital commerce and purchases from countries outsid

item.title: City Council, Stockholm, June 3, 2026.
item.description: The City of Stockholm plans to implement digital personnel records for all of its departments and will invest 70 million Swedish krona in this initiative. Additionally, a building in Frihamnen, called Magasin 5, is set to change ownership f

item.title: The Swedish National Audit Agency's report on the state's efforts to address problems related to gambling.
item.description: The Swedish National Audit Office believes that the government's efforts to reduce gambling problems are not working effectively enough. The government agrees with this criticism but does not believe that major changes are necessary at this

item.title: A good working environment in a changing working life – the government's working environment strategy for 2026–2031.
item.description: The government has developed a plan outlining the future of working conditions in Sweden between the years 2026 and 2031. The goal is for all workers to have a safe and healthy workplace where they do not get injured or become ill due to th

item.title: Sweden's strategy for a peaceful, secure, and sustainable Arctic.
item.description: The Swedish government has presented a plan for how the Arctic region can be kept safe and peaceful. The plan also focuses on protecting the environment and ensuring that the people who live there have good living conditions.

item.title: Reporting on the activities of the AP Funds through 2025.
item.description: The government has announced how the Swedish pension funds have performed up to the year 2025. The most important point is that the funds have generated 122 billion Swedish kronor in profits, but the strong Swedish krona meant that the prof

item.title: Decommissioning of the Payment Agency's system with transaction accounts.
item.description: The government wants to eliminate the system of special accounts at the Payment Agency. The purpose is for the agency to focus solely on preventing incorrect payments of money.

item.title: New opportunities to combat online recruitment.
item.description: The government wants to introduce a new law to prevent criminals from recruiting young people online. Those who own the digital platforms should be able to quickly be compelled to remove harmful content.

item.title: Additional budget adjustments for 2026 – Support for Ukraine, and support for households and other measures related to the war in the Middle East.
item.description: The government proposes allocating new funds in the 2026 budget to assist Ukraine with military equipment, and to support Swedish households and businesses facing high energy and fuel prices.

item.title: A revised abortion law.
item.description: The abortion law is being modernized. It clarifies that a pregnant woman can have an abortion up to and including the 18th week of pregnancy, that abortions should be provided promptly, and the requirement that abortions must be performed i

item.title: Supplementary provisions to EU regulations on chemicals and waste.
item.description: Sweden intends to implement stricter regulations regarding chemicals and waste in order to comply with new EU laws. It may become punishable to fail to properly label hazardous substances when they are transferred into containers.

item.title: City Council, Stockholm Municipality, May 25, 2026.
item.description: The City of Stockholm has made numerous decisions regarding new construction projects, parks, and protected natural areas. These decisions include plans for new housing, new roads, and the preservation of nature in various parts of the city

item.title: Enhanced protection against foreign nationals who pose significant security threats.
item.description: The government wants to amend the law to better protect Sweden from individuals who pose a threat to the country's security. The proposal includes measures that would make it easier to detain individuals and increase the penalties for certa

item.title: Expanded Powers for the Swedish Tax Agency within Population Registration Activities.
item.description: A new crime is being introduced: "facilitating false registration in the population register." This applies to anyone who knowingly contributes to inaccurate information being used in the population register, with the intent of making money

item.title: A government-issued electronic identification.
item.description: The government intends to introduce a new national e-identification system that will serve as a secure digital ID. This is being done to comply with EU regulations and to ensure that everyone in Sweden can digitally identify themselves in a

item.title: Agreement on Enhanced Partnership and Cooperation between the European Union and its Member States, on the one hand, and the Republic of Uzbekistan, on the other hand.
item.description: Sweden and the EU aim to establish a new and more significant agreement with the country of Uzbekistan. The purpose is to enhance cooperation in both political and economic matters between the two countries.

item.title: Agreement on Enhanced Partnership and Cooperation between the European Union and its Member States, on the one hand, and the Republic of Kyrgyzstan, on the other hand.
item.description: Sweden and the EU want to establish a new and more important agreement with the country of Kyrgyzstan. This new agreement aims to increase cooperation between the EU and Kyrgyzstan in areas such as politics and economics.

item.title: Sampling of data on household debt.
item.description: The government wants to amend the law so that the Central Bureau of Statistics (SCB) can collect more information about the amount of debt held by households, as well as the interest rates and repayments they are making. This is being done

item.title: Stricter regulations regarding supervision and storage.
item.description: The government wants to change the rules regarding how individuals from other countries who are in Sweden are checked or detained. The aim is to make the migration process more transparent and secure.

item.title: Stricter and clearer requirements regarding conduct for residence permits.
item.description: The "conduct" (lifestyle) of a foreign national should be given greater weight when residency permits are being considered and revoked. The purpose is to make it easier to deny or withdraw permits and to deport individuals with deficiencies

item.title: Enhanced return process.
item.description: The government aims to streamline the process of enforcing deportation and expulsion decisions. Several agencies, including the Swedish Social Insurance Agency (Försäkringskassan), the Swedish Tax Agency (Skatteverket), and the Swedish Pris

item.title: Revocation of permanent residence permits and adaptation of Swedish law to the EU's migration and asylum pact.
item.description: The government calls it a paradigm shift in migration policy. The possibility of obtaining permanent residency is being removed from the immigration law, and Swedish regulations are being aligned with the EU's migration and asylum pact, as

item.title: A more effective regulation of the ethical review process for research involving human subjects.
item.description: The government wants to change the rules for how research involving human subjects is regulated, in order to make the process more streamlined. Certain research projects that handle sensitive personal data will no longer require formal appr

item.title: Increased transparency in political processes.
item.description: The government wants to change the laws to make it easier for everyone to see how political decisions are made and how political parties obtain their funding. The proposal aims to stop anonymous donations and require those who attempt to in

item.title: Improved conditions for operational military cooperation.
item.description: In order for Sweden to fully participate in NATO's operational defense, it is proposed that the government be authorized to allow NATO, or a NATO or EU member state, to conduct military defense operations within Swedish territory. Several l

item.title: More integrated care for individuals with harmful substance use or addiction, and other psychiatric conditions.
item.description: Care for individuals with substance use disorders and mental health issues needs to be better integrated. Regions should ensure that addiction care is coordinated with other psychiatric services. Requirements for individual care plans will

item.title: National Planning for Transport Infrastructure 2026–2037
item.description: The government has established long-term plans for how roads and railways in Sweden will function between the years 2026 and 2037. The plan focuses on investing wisely in transportation and making the system more environmentally friendly.

item.title:
Requirements for the case management systems of municipal land surveying authorities.
# Krav på kommunala lantmäterimyndigheters ärendehanteringssystem
## Inledning
Detta dokument beskriver kraven på ärendehanteringssystem som används av kommunala lantmäterimyndigheter. Syftet är att säkerställa en effektiv, transparent och rättssäker hantering av lantmäterifrågor.
## Allmänna krav
* Systemet ska vara användarvänligt och lätt att lära sig. * Systemet ska vara säkert och skydda känslig information. * Systemet ska vara tillgängligt för alla berörda parter, inklusive allmänheten. * Systemet ska vara kompatibelt med andra system som används av kommunen. * Systemet ska vara skalbart för att kunna hantera framtida behov.
## Specifika krav
* Systemet ska kunna hantera alla typer av lantmäterifrågor, inklusive fastighetsbildning, samfälligheter och servitut. * Systemet ska kunna hantera alla steg i ärendeprocessen, från inledande ansökan till slutgiltigt beslut. * Systemet ska kunna generera alla nödvändiga dokument, inklusive ansökningar, beslut och protokoll. * Systemet ska kunna spåra alla åtgärder som vidtas i ett ärende. * Systemet ska kunna generera rapporter och statistik om ärendehanteringen. * Systemet ska kunna integreras med andra system, såsom geografiska informationssystem (GIS) och fastighetsregister.
## Säkerhet
* Systemet ska vara skyddat mot obehörig åtkomst. * Systemet ska ha en robust backup- och återställningsmekanism. * Systemet ska vara skyddat mot virus och annan skadlig programvara. * Systemet ska ha en revisionslogg som registrerar alla ändringar som görs i systemet.
## Tillgänglighet
* Systemet ska vara tillgängligt dygnet runt, sju dagar i veckan. * Systemet ska vara tillgängligt från alla typer av enheter, inklusive datorer, surfplattor och smartphones. * Systemet ska vara tillgängligt för personer med funktionsnedsättningar.
## Kompatibilitet
* Systemet ska vara kompatibelt med alla operativsystem som används av kommunen. * Systemet ska vara kompatibelt med alla webbläsare som används av kommunen. * Systemet ska vara kompatibelt med alla andra system som används av kommunen.
## Skalbarhet
* Systemet ska kunna hantera en ökning av antalet ärenden. * Systemet ska kunna hantera en ökning av antalet användare. * Systemet ska kunna hantera en ökning av datamängden.
## Integration
* Systemet ska kunna integreras med GIS-system. * Systemet ska kunna integreras med fastighetsregister. * Systemet ska kunna integreras med andra system som används av kommunen.
## Slutord
Dessa krav är avsedda att vara en vägledning för utvecklingen och implementeringen av ärendehanteringssystem för kommunala lantmäterimyndigheter. Det är viktigt att kraven anpassas till de specifika behoven hos varje kommun.
item.description: Municipalities that handle land surveying matters will be required to use the same digital system as the national authority, Lantmäteriet, starting in 2027. This is being implemented to ensure that the technology functions seamlessly betwee

item.title: Over-the-counter medications that require special counseling.
item.description: More medications could become available over-the-counter if people can speak with an expert at the pharmacy. This proposal aims to make it easier to access medication while still ensuring safety for both people and animals.
